frameworks.name: temporal difference learning
frameworks.one_line: TD learning with ITI state representation explains dopamine and behavior

frameworks.name: contingency-based account
frameworks.one_line: Traditional contingency definition fails to fit observed dopamine and licking

frameworks.name: ANCCR causal learning model
frameworks.one_line: Novel causal learning model also conflicts with the data

frameworks.name: recurrent neural network
frameworks.one_line: RNNs trained under TD develop state representations matching handcrafted model

core_question: How does prospective contingency between stimulus and outcome shape behavior and ventral striatal dopamine signals during Pavlovian learning?
key_assumptions: Ventral striatal dopamine reflects associative learning signals
Pavlovian contingency degradation is informative about stimulus-outcome prediction
An appropriate ITI state representation is required for TD models to fit
manipulations: Delivery of additional rewards uncued vs cued
key_findings: Anticipatory licking decreased when extra rewards were uncued but not when cued
Dopamine responses to the conditioned stimulus decreased only with uncued extra rewards
Traditional contingency accounts and ANCCR fail to explain the observed pattern
TD models with suitable ITI state representation reproduce behavior and dopamine
interpretations: TD error can simultaneously describe contingency effects and dopaminergic activity
Learned state representations, including those discovered by RNNs, bridge contingency and TD signals
